results.bodySnippet: OK - Connected and processing trade transactions successfully.

Error 422 (Unprocessable Entity): Compliance validation rejected. ESMA Trade Repository NACK rate is 12.4% (Threshold: <1.0%). Code 'ERR-VAL-509: Missing or Invalid CFI Code' on commodity swap trades due to upstream capture engine release v14.2 bug.

issues.title: High ESMA Trade Repository NACK Rate
issues.description: ESMA Trade Repository NACK rate is 12.4%, exceeding the critical limit of 1.0% due to missing or invalid CFI code validation errors.
issues.possibleCause: Upstream trade-capture-engine release v14.2 code drift omitting CFI codes on commodity swap trade contracts.

remediations.title: Rollback Upstream Trade Capture Release
remediations.explanation: Rolls back trade-capture-engine to compliant build version v14.1.

executiveSummary: [Heuristic SRE Engine] High ESMA Trade Repository feedback NACK rate (12.4%) due to missing CFI codes in trade submission payloads. Charlie identified a code validator bug in release v14.2. Redeploying upstream release v14.1 is recommended.

agentDialogues.content: Workgroup initialized. Alice here. I'm establishing the MCP tunnel to "Mifid FX and CMD" and loading cluster metadata. Bob, Charlie, David, Ethan — let's lock in.
Recon active. ESMA feedback route shows a critical NACK rejection spike. 12.4% of trades are failing compliance validation.

Charlie here. Audited git commits on compliance-engine. Release v14.2 was deployed recently. Look at this commit diff: 'cfiCode = trade.type === Swap ? null : trade.cfiCode'. It truncates swap CFI codes completely!

I checked the SRE History Database and found an identical incident occurred in application 'Mifid FX and CMD' on 2026-07-14. In that past incident, we resolved the issue by executing the 'redeploy_upstream_release' remediation which successfully returned health to 100%. I recommend we immediately prioritize this same action.
Brilliant diagnostics. This is a severe compliance infraction. Let's trigger the 'redeploy_upstream_release' to roll back trade-capture-engine to v14.1. I'm drafting the fix PR immediately.
Fully support the rollback. The DB has no lock states, it's strictly a schema validation error on trade inputs.

pullRequest.title: Fix: Restore missing CFI code mappings for commodity swaps
pullRequest.description: Fixes upstream trade validator bug that mistakenly mapped swap CFI codes to null, resulting in ESMA rejections.

results.bodySnippet: OK - Connected and processing trade transactions successfully.

Error 500 (Internal Server Error): Sybase Database exclusive transaction table lock detected on 'MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES' by SPID 892. Lock duration: 320 seconds. 1,402 credit trades pending persistence. Long-running manual batch blocking tables.

issues.title: Sybase Exclusive Transaction Table Lock on MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES
issues.description: Sybase Database exclusive transaction table lock detected on 'MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES' by SPID 892. Lock duration: 320 seconds. 1,402 credit trades pending persistence due to a long-running manual batch blocking tables.
issues.possibleCause: A rogue manual reporting batch run has acquired an exclusive table lock on MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES instead of using a row-level lock or a read-uncommitted isolation level.

remediations.title: Kill Blocking SPID on Sybase Cluster
remediations.explanation: Force kills the blocking SPID 892 thread, instantly releasing locked reporting rows to allow pending trades to persist.

executiveSummary: The 'Mifid Credits' application is experiencing a CRITICAL database lock on the 'MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES' table, caused by SPID 892. This has halted 1,402 credit trades from being persisted, threatening compliance with ESMA/MiFID II reporting timelines. Historical context confirms this is identical to incident run-hist-002. The immediate mitigation is to terminate the blocking SPID 892 (AMBER risk). A permanent fix has been proposed via a PR to enforce query timeouts and proper isolation levels on the batch processor.

agentDialogues.content: Workgroup initialized. Alice here. I'm establishing the MCP tunnel to "Mifid Credits" and loading cluster metadata. Bob, Charlie, David, Ethan — let's lock in.

Alert triggered on GKE cluster for app 'Mifid Credits'. The `/api/demo/regops/credits/db` endpoint is throwing HTTP 500. Latency is high at 156ms. Looking at the logs, I see: 'Sybase Database exclusive transaction table lock detected on MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES by SPID 892.' There are already 1,402 credit trades blocked in the queue. This is a severe threat to our real-time trade reporting limits!

agentDialogues.role: Incident History Database & SRE Knowledge Base
agentDialogues.content: I checked the SRE History Database and found an identical incident occurred in application 'Mifid Credits' on 2026-07-12T14:22:10.000Z. In that past incident, we resolved the issue by executing the 'kill_blocking_spid' remediation which successfully returned health to 100%. I recommend we immediately prioritize this same action.

I've queried active sessions on the Sybase cluster. SPID 892 has been active for over 320 seconds holding an exclusive lock on 'MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES'. It matches a manual batch export trigger. Terminating this SPID will roll back the manual batch run but will immediately free up the transaction block. I recommend we proceed with the kill command immediately.

While Bob cleans up the database state, I searched the repository for why these manual batches don't yield locks. I found that `batch-reporter-config.xml` lacks an explicit query timeout and is utilizing serializable transaction isolation instead of read-committed snapshot isolation. Let me patch this so future batch runs don't lock the entire OLTP table.

agentDialogues.role: RegOps Compliance, Release Guard, and Automated Remediation
agentDialogues.content: Killing SPID 892 is classified as AMBER because it aborts active batch reporting. However, keeping 1,402 trades in a pending state violates ESMA reporting timelines, which is a severe compliance infraction (RED limit). Therefore, the risk of killing the SPID is highly acceptable. I authorize `execute_automated_remediation` with the command `kill_blocking_spid`. I will also prepare a Pull Request using Charlie's suggested isolation level adjustments.

agentDialogues.role: Incident History Database & SRE Knowledge Base
agentDialogues.content: Understood. The remediation command `kill_blocking_spid` was executed successfully. SPID 892 is terminated, and the 1,402 queued credit trades are now persisting. Database lock duration has dropped to 0. I will update our knowledge weights to reinforce this resolution path for any future occurrences of 'Sybase DB Exclusive Transaction Lock'.
I've determined this cannot be fixed without a source code change. I have created Jira issue [ENG-190](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/ENG-190) with the investigation details and code suggestions.

pullRequest.title: Fix: Update database isolation level and add query timeout for batch reporting
pullRequest.description: Adjusts isolation levels in the batch reporter configuration to prevent exclusive table locks on MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES. Limits transaction blocks to read-committed snapshot-like isolation and enforces a strict query timeout of 30 seconds.

issues.title: Sybase DB Exclusive Transaction Lock on MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES
issues.description: Sybase Database exclusive transaction table lock detected on 'MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES' by SPID 892. Lock duration is currently 320 seconds, with 1,402 credit trades pending persistence due to a long-running manual batch blocking tables.
issues.possibleCause: A manual reporting batch job was executed without an isolation level constraint, locking the transaction table and blocking all real-time credit trade persistence.

remediations.title: Kill Blocking SPID on Sybase Cluster
remediations.explanation: Force kills the blocking SPID 892 thread, instantly releasing locked reporting rows and clearing the queue.

executiveSummary: The Mifid Credits application fell into a CRITICAL state due to an exclusive Sybase table lock on 'MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES' held by SPID 892. This halted 1,402 critical credit trades. By identifying a historical resolution pattern, the automated agent workgroup executed an AMBER-rated mitigation to kill the blocking SPID, restoring full system health, and drafted a permanent SQL patch to prevent future locks.

agentDialogues.content: Workgroup initialized. Alice here. I'm establishing the MCP tunnel to "Mifid Credits" and loading cluster metadata. Bob, Charlie, David, Ethan — let's lock in.

Alert triggered! The endpoint `/api/demo/regops/credits/db` is returning a 500 Internal Server Error with high latency. The payload indicates a Sybase Database exclusive transaction lock on `MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES` by SPID 892. It has been active for 320 seconds, and we have 1,402 credit trades pending persistence. GKE cluster infrastructure looks green, but our database layer is severely blocked. Bob, can you analyze database thread activity?

agentDialogues.role: Incident History Database & SRE Knowledge Base
agentDialogues.content: I checked the SRE History Database and found an identical incident occurred in application 'Mifid Credits' on 2026-07-12T14:22:10.000Z. In that past incident, we resolved the issue by executing the 'kill_blocking_spid' remediation which successfully returned health to 100%. I recommend we immediately prioritize this same action.

I've run the active monitors and confirmed that SPID 892 is holding an exclusive write lock on the `MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES` table. It matches a manual reporting batch execution. It has backed up 1,402 trade insertions. To restore service immediately, I support Ethan's recommendation to kill SPID 892. Charlie, do you see any code drifts or manual queries committed recently that would trigger this?

I scanned the repository and there have been no recent production deployments today. However, checking `src/main/resources/db/migration/V2.1__manual_batch_query.sql`, I noticed we are using raw `HOLDLOCK` in the manual reporting query. This requests an exclusive lock that blocks trade processing. I'm drafting a pull request to change this to `WITH (NOLOCK)` so this doesn't lock tables again. David, can you evaluate the operational remediation risk?

agentDialogues.role: RegOps Compliance, Release Guard, and Automated Remediation
agentDialogues.content: Understood. The 1,402 pending trades threaten our regulatory reporting SLAs. Under ESMA rules, we must resolve this bottleneck immediately. I classify the operational runbook execution `kill_blocking_spid` as an AMBER risk. It is safe because it only terminates the non-critical manual reporting thread. I've initiated the automation scripts. I have also verified Charlie's proposed pull request PR-903 to replace the locking mechanism. I will execute the kill now.

Remediation executed! Checking GKE pods and database logs... Yes! SPID 892 has been terminated successfully. The 1,402 trade queue is clearing rapidly, and latency on `/api/demo/regops/credits/db` is down to 20ms with 200 OK statuses. Systems are fully restored. Excellent teamwork!
I've determined this cannot be fixed without a source code change. I have created Jira issue [ENG-196](https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/ENG-196) with the investigation details and code suggestions.

pullRequest.title: Fix: Use shared non-blocking locks for Mifid Credits manual reporting batch
pullRequest.description: Changes the database transaction query hint in manual_batch_query.sql from HOLDLOCK to WITH (NOLOCK) to prevent exclusive table locks on MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES table.

filesChanged.originalCode: SELECT * FROM MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES HOLDLOCK WHERE processed = 0;
filesChanged.modifiedCode: SELECT * FROM MIFID_CREDIT_TRADES WITH (NOLOCK) WHERE processed = 0;

results.bodySnippet: Error 503 (Service Unavailable): IBM MQ Queue 'MIFID.RATES.IN' depth exceeded critical threshold of 50,000 (Current: 78,401 msgs). Outbound regulatory transmission gateway 'rates-tx-prod-918' has stale TCP socket handles. Reset required.
OK - Connected and processing trade transactions successfully.

issues.title: IBM MQ Queue Depth Limit Exceeded on MIFID.RATES.IN
issues.description: IBM MQ Queue 'MIFID.RATES.IN' depth has reached 78,401 messages, far exceeding the critical compliance threshold of 50,000. Outbound regulatory transmission gateway 'rates-tx-prod-918' has hung with stale TCP socket handles.
issues.possibleCause: Stale TCP socket handles on the outbound transmission gateway 'rates-tx-prod-918' are blocking message egress, causing a backlog in the queue.

remediations.title: Reset Outbound Regulatory TCP Socket Handles
remediations.explanation: Re-binds the socket consumers, unblocking transmission flow immediately and clearing queue backup.

executiveSummary: An outage occurred in the Mifid Rates application where the queue 'MIFID.RATES.IN' backed up to 78,401 messages due to stale TCP socket handles on the gateway 'rates-tx-prod-918'. The database remains fully functional. A historical audit revealed an identical incident was previously resolved safely by executing a reset of the outbound regulatory socket handles. This automated remediation has been scheduled to restore message flow immediately.

agentDialogues.content: Workgroup initialized. Alice here. I'm establishing the MCP tunnel to "Mifid Rates" and loading cluster metadata. Bob, Charlie, David, Ethan — let's lock in.

Alert triggered on GKE cluster! The endpoint `/api/demo/regops/rates/queue` is throwing a 503 Service Unavailable. Pod metrics indicate that network transmission rates have flatlined. According to container stderr logs, the IBM MQ Queue 'MIFID.RATES.IN' depth has ballooned to 78,401 messages, breaching our 50,000 safety threshold. It points to a stale TCP socket handle on 'rates-tx-prod-918'. Bob, is this causing any backpressure or transactional locks on our SQL layer?

I've run a diagnostics check on the PostgreSQL instance using `pg_stat_activity_query` and `pg_locks_monitor`. The `/api/demo/regops/rates/db` endpoint is operating flawlessly with an average latency of 20ms and 100% success rate. No transaction stalls or exclusive table locks are active. This is strictly isolated to the egress networking and socket layer on the message broker. Over to you, Ethan—do we have historical runbooks for this 'rates-tx-prod-918' stale socket?

agentDialogues.role: Incident History Database & SRE Knowledge Base
agentDialogues.content: I checked the SRE History Database and found an identical incident occurred in application 'Mifid Rates' on 2026-07-10T08:15:22.000Z. In that past incident, we resolved the issue by executing the 'reset_regulatory_socket' remediation which successfully returned health to 100%. I recommend we immediately prioritize this same action.

I scanned recent deployment manifests and configuration files using `ast_syntax_scanner`. There have been no new code deployments or configuration drift in the gateway repository over the last 48 hours. This confirms it is a transient TCP network state issue rather than an application logic error. A physical patch is unnecessary. David, can we safely trigger the automated command?

agentDialogues.role: RegOps Compliance, Release Guard, and Automated Remediation
agentDialogues.content: Excellent analysis. Given that the queue backlog impacts ESMA/MiFID regulatory reporting timelines, this is highly compliance-sensitive. Fortunately, the remediation `reset_regulatory_socket` is pre-approved and carries a GREEN risk classification. I am executing `execute_automated_remediation` with the command parameters now to re-bind the outbound sockets on 'rates-tx-prod-918'. Let's verify health after execution.

Command execution successful! Outbound socket handles have re-established connections. Latency on `/api/demo/regops/rates/queue` has dropped to 45ms, and message transmission has resumed. The queue depth has already dropped to 12,000 and is rapidly draining to zero. Ethan, please update the learning weights to catalog this successful recovery.

pullRequest.title: No Code Change Required
pullRequest.description: This incident was resolved purely at the infrastructure level via socket reset. No code patch was needed.

Our incident log confirms that a similar 'CFI Code missing' anomaly happened in 2025 during an FX deployment. We solved it back then by a quick hotfix patch in pull request. I suggest David immediately drafts a patch PR.
